title: CODEX® Multiplexed Imaging | Tissue Sectioning
author: Diane Saunders
materials: Gather reagents: Poly-l-lysine, 0.1% (wt/vol) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P8920 22 x 22m Glass Cover Slips # 1 1/2 Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #72204-01
Slowly pipet off the poly-L-lysine solution and dispose.

steps.title: Gently place coverslips at the bottom...
steps.instruction: Gently place coverslips at the bottom of a 500-mL glass beaker and swirl to spread the coverslips out. Add approximately 20 mL poly-L-lysine solution to the beaker, ensuring coverslips are fully submerged. Rotate the beaker at a 45° angle for 00:01:00 to mix.

steps.title: Set the cryostat temperature to -20 °C
steps.instruction: Set the cryostat temperature to -20 °C . Remove a fresh blade, clean with ethanol and a lint free wipe as needed, and insert blade.

steps.title: Using OCT or CMC, mount sample...
steps.instruction: Using OCT or CMC, mount sample onto the cryostat chuck in the desired orientation. Trim CMC using larger section increments of 30-50 μm until the tissue becomes visible ( Figure 1a ). Figure 1. Sectioning cryopreserved tissue onto CODEX coverslip.

steps.title: 3D printed coverslip holders
steps.instruction: 3D printed coverslip holders: Codex Rack MkIII-Body.stl We use these with the following: PolarSafe™ PCR Cardboard Freezer Boxes Fisher Scientific Catalog #10-987-065 7x8 Double Zipper Bags Uline Catalog #S-20325 6x9 Double Zipper Bags Uline Catalog #S-17788

steps.title: Spread WypAll® towels on the benchtop
steps.instruction: Spread WypAll® towels on the benchtop. In small batches, remove coverslips from the beaker, separate from one another, and place individual coverslips side by side on towels. Layer another towel on top and gently dab to remove excess liquid. Leave on benchtop until completely dry. ⓘ Coated coverslips can be stored in a petri dish for up to 2 months.

steps.title: go to step #5 and repeat...
steps.instruction: go to step #5 and repeat for a total of 5-7 washes.

steps.title: Fill the beaker containing the coverslips...
steps.instruction: Fill the beaker containing the coverslips to half volume with Milli-Q® water. Swirl gently, let sit for 00:00:30 , and then slowly pour off water into the sink.

steps.title: Cover beaker with parafilm and incubate...
steps.instruction: Cover beaker with parafilm and incubate for minimum of 12:00:00 at Room temperature . Coverslips can sit in the poly-L-lysine solution for up to one week.

steps.title: Obtain 6 µm to 10 µm...
steps.instruction: Obtain 6 µm to 10 µm sections, using a fine-tipped paintbrush to guide the section onto base and place coverslip onto section ( Figure 1b-1d ).

steps.title: Using forceps, slot coverslips with mounted...
steps.instruction: Using forceps, slot coverslips with mounted tissue into a plastic coverslip holder under the appropriate donor/block ID label(s).

steps.title: Store coverslips at -80 °C for up to 6 months
steps.instruction: Store coverslips at -80 °C for up to 6 months.

steps.title: As necessary, mount adjacent sections onto...
steps.instruction: As necessary, mount adjacent sections onto Gold Plus slides (for traditional immunohistochemistry) and/or ITO slides (for mass spectrometry). Record all sections cut, the thickness, and any additional block shaved off in the process.
